result:
## Summary

The issue was in `AnnotationRule.kt` at `/tmp/project-pinterest__ktlint-1849/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`.

**Root cause:** The `visitAnnotationEntry` method had three checks that could trigger formatting changes on annotation entries. The first check (line 69-76) already excluded annotations inside `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST` (for annotations with parameters), but the other two checks did not:

1. **`checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`** (lines 78-82): Triggered when multiple annotations appeared on the same line (e.g., `@Valid @NotNull`), forcing them onto separate lines. This was not excluded for `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST`.

2. **`checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct`** (lines 84-89): Triggered when multiple annotations were on the same line as the annotated construct (e.g., `@Valid @NotNull FooDto`), inserting a newline before the construct. This was also not excluded for `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST`.

**Fix:** Added `!node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST)` guards to both checks, consistent with the existing guard on the first check. This prevents the `AnnotationRule` from forcing line breaks between annotations and their annotated types when they appear inside generic type argument lists like `List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>`.
